name: Bihar Labour Laws
overview: State-specific labour law requirements applicable to establishments operating in Bihar. Independent state labour laws continue unless specifically repealed or replaced. Following commencement of the four central Labour Codes on 21 November 2025, each obligation must be mapped to the appropriate government and the corresponding Central, State or Union Territory rules and notifications. This record does not treat local Shops and Establishments, professional-tax, labour-welfare, holiday or leave requirements as automatically superseded.

authority.name: Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar

rules.title: Review Bihar Shops and Establishments Registration
rules.description: Determine whether registration under the Bihar Shops and Establishments legislation is applicable.

citation.reference: Applicable Bihar Shops and Establishments legislation

rules.title: Maintain Statutory Registers and Employment Records
rules.description: Maintain all registers, employment records and notices prescribed under Bihar labour legislation. Maintain any additional electronic or physical records, wage slips, notices, returns and audit trails required under an applicable Labour Code, saved instrument or rule issued by the appropriate government.

rules.title: Display Statutory Notices
rules.description: Display all statutory notices required under Bihar labour laws at the workplace. Maintain any additional electronic or physical records, wage slips, notices, returns and audit trails required under an applicable Labour Code, saved instrument or rule issued by the appropriate government.

rules.title: Review Working Hours, Leave and Weekly Holiday Requirements
rules.description: Ensure compliance with Bihar-specific provisions governing working hours, overtime, leave and weekly holidays. Where the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020 applies to the establishment or worker category, assess its working-hours, rest, overtime, annual-leave, safety and welfare requirements separately from independent State or Union Territory law.

citation.reference: Applicable Bihar Shops and Establishments Rules
Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020
citation.section: Working-hours, annual-leave, safety and welfare provisions

rules.title: Review Labour Welfare Fund Requirements
rules.description: Review whether any Bihar-specific labour welfare obligations or contributions apply to the establishment.

rules.title: Determine the Appropriate Government for Each Labour Code Obligation
rules.description: For each establishment, legal entity, industry and worker category, determine whether the Central Government or the relevant State or Union Territory Government is the appropriate government. Apply the corresponding rules, notifications, wage rates, schemes, registrations, licences, forms, portals and enforcement authorities instead of assuming that the Central Rules apply universally.

citation.reference: Implementation of the four Labour Codes from 21 November 2025
Appropriate-government and State rule-making provisions under the four Labour Codes

rules.title: Apply the Code on Wages and Current Wage Notifications
rules.description: For wage periods on or after 21 November 2025, assess minimum wages, timely payment, authorised deductions, overtime, gender-based wage equality, statutory bonus and wage records under the Code on Wages, 2019. Apply the rules and notifications issued by the appropriate government, and retain a legacy notification only to the extent saved by section 69 and not later replaced or repealed.

citation.section: Chapters II to IV and section 69

citation.reference: Labour Code implementation and applicable wage-rule notifications

rules.title: Assess Industrial Relations, Social Security and OSHWC Code Requirements
rules.description: Assess threshold-based and event-based duties under the Industrial Relations Code, 2020, the Code on Social Security, 2020 and the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020. The assessment must cover industrial-relations forums and workforce actions, provident fund and insurance schemes, gratuity, maternity benefit, employee compensation, building-worker welfare, occupational safety, registration, appointment letters, working conditions, contract labour and inter-State migrant workers where applicable.

citation.reference: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020

rules.title: Preserve Independent Local Laws and Saved Legacy Instruments
rules.description: Continue to comply with independent State or Union Territory Shops and Establishments, professional-tax, labour-welfare, holiday, leave and other local laws unless they are lawfully repealed or replaced. Preserve earlier rules, notifications, registrations, licences, exemptions, claims, liabilities, proceedings and actions under repealed central enactments only to the extent saved by the corresponding Labour Code and not inconsistent with it.

citation.reference: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020

recommendations.title: Annual Labour Compliance Audit
recommendations.description: Conduct a yearly audit covering labour registrations, statutory records and employment documentation.

recommendations.title: Digital Statutory Register Management
recommendations.description: Maintain digital statutory registers and a compliance calendar for inspections and renewals.

recommendations.title: Maintain an Appropriate Government Matrix
recommendations.description: Map each establishment, industry, worker category and compliance topic to the Central Government or the competent State or Union Territory Government, with the applicable rules, notifications, portals and authorities.

recommendations.title: Maintain a Labour Code Instrument Register
recommendations.description: Track each Labour Code rule, draft, final notification, wage revision, scheme, form, exemption, authority appointment and replacement of a saved legacy instrument.

recommendations.title: Review Official Notifications Monthly
recommendations.description: Review official Ministry and Bihar Government publications monthly for Labour Code rules, amendments, wage notifications, schemes, thresholds, forms and enforcement appointments.

sources.name: Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar
sources.authority: Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar

sources.name: Ministry of Labour & Employment Labour Codes Portal
sources.authority: Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India

sources.name: India Code
sources.authority: Legislative Department, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India

governance.changeSummary: Updated the Bihar record for implementation of the four Labour Codes without superseding independent state laws. Preserved the existing local rules and identifiers, added appropriate-government, Code on Wages, Industrial Relations, Social Security, OSHWC and repeal-and-savings controls, and added monthly monitoring of official implementation instruments.

exceptions.title: Independent State Laws Continue
exceptions.description: The four Labour Codes do not by themselves repeal independent Bihar Shops and Establishments, professional-tax, labour-welfare, holiday, leave or other local laws outside the central enactments expressly repealed by the Codes.

exceptions.title: Central Rules Are Jurisdiction Dependent
exceptions.description: Central Labour Code Rules apply where the Central Government is the appropriate government. Where the State or Union Territory Government is the appropriate government, use its valid final rules, notifications, schemes, forms and appointed authorities. Draft rules must not be treated as final law.

exceptions.title: Saved Legacy Instruments Require Ongoing Review
exceptions.description: A rule, notification, registration, licence, exemption, claim, proceeding or action under a repealed central enactment continues only to the extent preserved by the relevant Labour Code and until it is lawfully replaced, repealed or superseded.
